task_description: Create a new text document named text4.txt in the current folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the Windows taskbar with a semitransparent red X centered on the yellow File Explorer icon, which has a small preview thumbnail above it showing an open File Explorer window. Nearby taskbar icons include Microsoft Store, iTunes, a green app icon, and Google Chrome. In the full screen, the Windows 11 desktop is visible with many shortcuts on the left, a folder named file1 near the upper right, and the taskbar at the bottom; the click target is a toolbar/taskbar control rather than a desktop object.
caption.action: Click the File Explorer taskbar icon under the red X.
caption.expectation: The File Explorer window should come to the foreground and become the active window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows only a light gray blank area with a semitransparent red X centered on empty space, indicating the target is not on an icon, file, or visible control. In the full screen, a File Explorer window for the folder named file1 is open in details view. The file list shows items such as .git, test, package.json, and several text documents, but the red X falls in the large empty white pane below the listed items. No context menu is currently visible, so this is a right-click on blank background space in the folder rather than on an object; no menu options can yet be summarized because none are shown.
caption.action: Right-click the empty white background area in the file list under the red X.
caption.expectation: A folder background context menu should appear with general view and sorting options plus folder-level actions, rather than actions for a specific file or folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a vertical submenu from File Explorer's context menu, with a semitransparent red X centered on the item labeled '文本文档' (Text Document). The item is a menu option in the New submenu, shown among other file types such as RTF, Microsoft Visio Drawing, and Excel worksheet entries. In the full screen, File Explorer is open to the folder 'file1', and a background context menu is open over the blank file list area with the New submenu expanded to the right; this is a menu command rather than a file in the folder.
caption.action: Click the '文本文档' (Text Document) option under the red X in the New submenu.
caption.expectation: A new text document should be created in the current folder, typically appearing in the file list with its name selected for immediate renaming.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the newly created text document in File Explorer with its filename in inline rename mode: the current name text 'text.txt' is highlighted inside a small editable text box, indicating the existing name will be replaced by typing. In the full screen, the File Explorer window is open to the 'file1' folder in details view, with other items such as .git, test, package.json, and several .txt files listed above; the selected new file row shows type '文本文档' and size '0 KB'.
caption.action: Type 'TEXT4' to replace the selected filename text.
caption.expectation: The editable filename should update to 'TEXT4' in the rename field, ready to be confirmed as the new document name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a blank light-gray area with a semitransparent red X, which does not indicate a specific visible control for this step. In the full screen, File Explorer is open to the folder named 'file1' in details view. A newly created text document is listed at the bottom of the file list with the name 'text4.txt' shown in a small inline edit box, and the row is selected. Other items such as .git, test, package.json, text1.txt, text2.txt, and text3.txt are visible above it.
caption.action: Press Enter to confirm the new filename 'text4.txt'.
caption.expectation: The rename box should close, leaving the new text document saved with the confirmed name 'text4.txt' and selected in the folder.
